reasoning: The lowest limit of the federal funds target range as of Feb 1, 2026, is 3.50%. The FOMC sets the target range at scheduled meetings (or extremely rare emergency meetings). The next scheduled FOMC meeting for 2026 is on March 17-18. Since both resolution dates (Feb 8 and March 3) fall before the next scheduled meeting, the target rate will almost certainly remain unchanged at 3.50%. The likelihood of an inter-meeting emergency rate hike is historically negligible, particularly when the Fed has been in a cutting cycle over the past year. Accordingly, the probabilities of an increase by these dates are at the minimum allowable floor.
